key_insight: The paper introduces a domain-decomposed randomized neural network that splits near-field and far-field behavior to solve PDEs on unbounded domains efficiently.
engineering_relevance: This approach can reduce truncation artifacts and improve scalability for computational physics and engineering simulations with exterior or infinite-domain effects.
method: Assign separate randomized subnetworks to different spatial regions.
Couple subdomains through boundary and interface conditions.
Solve only output-layer coefficients via linear least-squares in Petrov--Galerkin or collocation form.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations, with theory covering approximation and optimization error components.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of domain decomposition and the conditioning/accuracy of the least-squares and quadrature discretizations.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
